title: Promote TFirestoreApi extension methods to virtual instance methods
blocks: All dummy api tasks — a subclass cannot override extension methods in Dart, so every method the dummy needs to intercept has to live on the class body first.

status_instructions.on_start: Update this file's `status` field to `in-progress` before any code edits, so no other agent picks the task up.
status_instructions.on_done: Update `status` to `done`, fill the `worklog` with a terse list of what was done, then `mv` this file to workspace/tasks/archive/dev/1-refactor/01-promote-firestore-api-extensions-to-instance-methods-task.json.
status_instructions.on_followup: Scan all sibling task files under workspace/tasks/dev/** and append clarifying notes to their `side_context` array only if your refactor revealed something the next agent must know (e.g. a helper's new signature, a subtle behavior you had to preserve). Never edit a task's original fields — only append to `side_context`.

description: The existing TFirestoreApi<T> class scatters its public operations across seven Dart extensions (create, delete, get, list, search, stream, update). Dart resolves extension method calls statically by the receiver's declared type, which means a subclass cannot override them. Before the dummy api can intercept any call, every extension method must be promoted to a virtual instance method on the TFirestoreApi<T> class body. Call sites, public exports, part file layout, and member ordering must remain externally identical.
context_for_junior: The package lib/apis/ folder has one main file (t_firestore_api.dart) plus seven part files. Each part file currently defines an `extension TurboFirestoreXxxApi<T> on TFirestoreApi<T> { ... }` block that attaches methods to the class from the outside. Dart has a quirk: when you write `api.createDoc(...)` where `api` is typed as `TFirestoreApi<T>`, the compiler picks the extension method at compile time based on the static type — it does not look at the runtime type of the object. That means if we later make a `TDummyFirestoreApi<T> extends TFirestoreApi<T>` and try to override `createDoc`, the override will never be called. The fix is to take every method body out of the `extension ... on TFirestoreApi<T> { ... }` wrapper and place it directly inside the class. Part files stay, they just become `part of t_firestore_api.dart` contributions that extend the class body. From the caller's perspective nothing changes — `api.createDoc(...)` still compiles and runs the same — but from the subclass's perspective, the methods are now virtual and overridable.
why_it_matters: Without this refactor every downstream task in this feature is physically impossible. This is a purely mechanical change but the test suite must stay green so we know we did not change observable behavior.
instructions: Read the spec section `prerequisite_refactor` and follow its `scope.keep_on_class` / `scope.promote_to_instance_methods` lists exactly — do not promote members not listed there, and do not remove any member listed under `keep_on_class`.

For each of the seven part files listed in the spec, rewrite the file so the `extension` wrapper is replaced by a `part of t_firestore_api.dart;` directive (if it is not already a part file, convert it), and move the method bodies as direct members into the class TFirestoreApi<T> via the part file.
Preserve every public signature exactly — parameter names, defaults, return types, documentation comments, @override annotations where present.
Keep private helpers (`_extractDocumentData`, `_buildFullPath`, `_createException`, `_logResultLength`, `_handleBatchResponse`, `_handleBatchOperation`) on the class exactly as they are now.
Keep class-level section header comments in sync with `dev-conventions/conventions/class-structure.md` ordering: constructor → DEPENDENCIES → STATE → UTIL → FETCHERS → HELPERS → MUTATORS → dispose.
Update t_firestore_api.dart's `part` declarations if any part file name or boundary changes.
Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api` and `make test package=turbo_firestore_api` from the monorepo root. Both must pass with zero warnings and zero test failures.
Do not delete any existing test, do not add new tests, do not touch services/, do not touch any sibling package.
goals: Every method the dummy api will later need to override is a virtual instance method on class TFirestoreApi<T>.
Zero public api change, zero call-site change, zero test change.
non_goals: No doc rewriting beyond moving comments with their methods.
No dummy api work — that belongs to later tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Every method listed under `prerequisite_refactor.scope.promote_to_instance_methods` is declared inside the class body of TFirestoreApi<T> (via its part file) and is no longer wrapped in an `extension ... on TFirestoreApi<T>` block.
Grep across lib/ and test/ reveals no `extension Turbo.*Api<T> on TFirestoreApi<T>` matches.
Public export surface of the package is byte-identical to before the refactor.
constraints: No workarounds, no `dynamic` escape hatches, no method body rewrites beyond relocation.
Trailing commas required, single quotes preferred, `const` constructors enforced (inherited from monorepo analysis rules).
No changes to CLAUDE.md, dependencies, or Melos config.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP file_search / get_code_structure / read_file for codebase exploration
Edit + Read for the relocation edits
verification: Grep for any remaining `extension Turbo.*Api<T> on TFirestoreApi<T>` — must be zero results.

quality_bar.maintainability: The class stays scannable — keep the part-file split so the single class body does not balloon into one unreadable file.
quality_bar.dry: Do not inline helper methods into their callers just because they are now in the same class.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: Each part file still owns exactly one operation family (create, delete, get, list, search, stream, update).
quality_bar.performance: No measurable change — this is a source reorganization only.
quality_bar.abuse_resistance: Zero — nothing here is exposed to end users.

worklog: Dart does not support splitting a class body across part files, so direct class-member promotion via part files is not syntactically possible. Used the closest valid Dart idiom: library-private base class (_TFirestoreApiBase<T>) + mixin composition.

Introduced abstract _TFirestoreApiBase<T> in t_firestore_api.dart holding all private state, constructor, helpers (keep_on_class members), and 4 abstract declarations for cross-mixin method resolution (getDocRefById, getDocRefByIdWithConverter, listCollectionReference, listCollectionReferenceWithConverter).
TFirestoreApi<T> now extends _TFirestoreApiBase<T> with all 7 operation-family mixins. Constructor uses super-parameter forwarding — zero public API change.
Converted all 7 part files from extension ... on TFirestoreApi<T> to mixin ... on _TFirestoreApiBase<T>. Method bodies unchanged.

Added @override on getDocRefById, getDocRefByIdWithConverter (t_firestore_get_api.dart) and listCollectionReference, listCollectionReferenceWithConverter (t_firestore_list_api.dart) since they now implement abstract base declarations.
Removed now-unused import of t_firestore_api.dart from example view file (extension methods no longer need explicit import — they are instance methods).

Files changed: lib/apis/t_firestore_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_create_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_delete_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_get_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_list_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_search_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_stream_api.dart, lib/apis/t_firestore_update_api.dart, example/lib/turbo_firestore_api/views/turbo_firestore_api/turbo_firestore_api_view.dart.
Grep for extension.*Api<T> on TFirestoreApi: 0 matches.
Public export barrel lib/turbo_firestore_api.dart: zero diff.
